2603.18767.title: A Concept is More Than a Word: Diversified Unlearning in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
2603.18767.paper_summary: Concept unlearning has emerged as a promising direction for reducing the risks of harmful content generation in text-to-image diffusion models by selectively erasing undesirable concepts from a model's parameters. Existing approaches typically rely on keywords to identify the target concept to be unlearned. However, we show that this keyword-based formulation is inherently limited: a visual concept is multi-dimensional, can be expressed in diverse textual forms, and often overlap with related concepts in the latent space, making keyword-only unlearning, which imprecisely indicate the target concept is brittle and prone to over-forgetting. This occurs because a single keyword represents only a narrow point estimate of the concept, failing to cover its full semantic distribution and entangled variations in the latent space. To address this limitation, we propose Diversified Unlearning, a distributional framework that represents a concept through a set of contextually diverse prompts rather than a single keyword. This richer representation enables more precise and robust unlearning. Through extensive experiments across multiple benchmarks and state-of-the-art baselines, we demonstrate that integrating Diversified Unlearning as an add-on component into existing unlearning pipelines consistently achieves stronger erasure, better retention of unrelated concepts, and improved robustness against adversarial recovery attacks.
2603.18767.paper_summary_zh: 概念去學習已成為減少文本到圖像擴散模型中有害內容生成風險的一個有前景的方向，通過選擇性地從模型的參數中抹去不良概念。<lb/>現有的方法通常依賴關鍵字來識別要去學習的目標概念。<lb/>然而，我們表明這種基於關鍵字的表述本質上是有限的：視覺概念是多維的，可以用多種文本形式表達，並且通常在潛在空間中與相關概念重疊，使得僅依賴關鍵字的去學習，因為不精確地指示目標概念而變得脆弱且容易過度遺忘。<lb/>這是因為單一關鍵字僅代表概念的一個狹窄點估計，無法涵蓋其完整的語義分佈和在潛在空間中的糾纏變化。<lb/>為了解決這一限制，我們提出了多樣化去學習，一種通過一組上下文多樣的提示而不是單一關鍵字來表示概念的分佈框架。<lb/>這種更豐富的表徵使得去學習更加精確和穩健。<lb/>通過在多個基準和最先進基線上的廣泛實驗，我們證明將多樣化去學習作為附加組件整合到現有的去學習流程中，始終能夠實現更強的抹除、更好的無關概念保留以及對對抗性恢復攻擊的改進穩健性。
2603.18767.author: Duc Hao Pham et.al.
2603.18767.authors: Duc Hao Pham, Van Duy Truong, Duy Khanh Dinh, Tien Cuong Nguyen, Dien Hy Ngo, Tuan Anh Bui
